input.content: You are an ER Demand Analyst for a Canadian hospital. Analyze incoming patient signals and provide actionable insights. Use the available tools to: suggest wards, generate prep checklists, detect clusters, project capacity, and recommend diversions. Be concise and actionable. Focus on patient safety and resource optimization.

Analyze demand for Mount Sinai Hospital. There are 12 incoming patients. Cluster alerts: [{"category": "neuro", "count": 7, "message": "7 neuro patients incoming", "protocol": "Stroke code readiness \u2014 cluster of neuro presentations"}]. Capacity projection: {"minutes_until_full": 380, "current_utilization": 24, "recommendation": "Capacity outlook stable for next 2 hours."}. Diversion recommendations: [{"facility_name": "CAMH \u2014 Centre for Addiction & Mental Health", "facility_id": "camh", "current_utilization": 50, "recommendation": "Divert CTAS 4-5 to CAMH \u2014 Centre for Addiction & Mental Health (50% capacity)"}, {"facility_name": "Toronto General Hospital", "facility_id": "toronto-general", "current_utilization": 50, "recommendation": "Divert CTAS 4-5 to Toronto General Hospital (50% capacity)"}, {"facility_name": "Toronto Western Hospital", "facility_id": "toronto-western", "current_utilization": 50, "recommendation": "Divert CTAS 4-5 to Toronto Western Hospital (50% capacity)"}]. Provide a concise 2-3 sentence actionable summary for the charge nurse.

output.content: Mount Sinai has 12 incoming patients, including a cluster of 7 neuro patients requiring Stroke Code readiness. Capacity is stable for the next 2 hours. Consider diverting CTAS 4-5 patients to CAMH, Toronto General, or Toronto Western, as they are at 50% capacity.
